name: Background Color Trigger
explanation:
Change the background colors by changing <color1> and <color2> in an RGB format over <fadeTime> seconds.

<radius> determines the player's distance from the trigger for it to activate. This is shown by the circle around the trigger.

name: Ground Color Trigger
explanation:
Change the ground colors by changing <color1> and <color2> in an RGB format over <fadeTime> seconds.

<radius> determines the player's distance from the trigger for it to activate. This is shown by the circle around the trigger.

name: Text
explanation:
Change <content> to change the text.

Change <color> in an RGB format to change the text's color.

Change <outline> from 0 to 4 to choose the font.

Change <width> to set word wrapping to N pixels. Leaving it at 0 disables word wrap.

name: Icon
explanation: Make the icon you want to use in the icon kit tab, then click the copy button (under the color picker), go back here, remove the old contents and paste what you copied here.

name: On/Off Switch
explanation:
When <physicsCanPress> is at 0, only the player can press the switch.

When it's at 1, only objects affected by gravity can press it.

When it's at 2, both the player and gravity objects can press it.

name: Force Trigger
explanation:
<force> determines the speed gravity-affected objects will have from the trigger.

<angle> changes the angle of the force in degrees (starting from what is usually 90 degrees). This is shown by the line going from the trigger.

<radius> determines the objects' distance from the trigger for it to apply force. This is shown by the circle around the trigger.

name: Tiled Rectangle
explanation:
Use this instead of placing a lot of decoration blocks individually for faster building and better performance.

<id> was changed when you chose the block to place, and no you can't use anything but what you were shown.

<width> and <height> (in blocks) are changed when you drag the block and release it, but you can also change them manually.
